populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in villages, grazing zones, and small regional market centers of the Guinea highlands, composed of Fulani, Jalonke, and other nearby peoples; density was moderate and supported by a stable mix of farming and herding

socialClasses.zh: 主要包括阿勒马米与教士贵族、地方首领与骑兵精英、自由农民牧民、商人和工匠，以及被役使人口与奴隶；穆斯林学识与家族出身显著影响流动机会
socialClasses.en: The principal social classes included the Almami and clerical nobility, local chiefs and cavalry elites, free farmers and pastoralists, merchants and artisans, and servile populations and slaves; Muslim learning and lineage strongly affected opportunities for mobility

allies.zh: 无严格意义上的固定国际盟友，主要依靠与周边穆斯林商人网络、部分富拉尼与曼德地方势力保持宗教和贸易合作
allies.en: It had no strict permanent international allies, relying instead on religious and commercial cooperation with neighboring Muslim merchant networks and some Fulani and Mande local powers

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是抵制其扩张和征税的周边高地社群、竞争性内陆政权以及在贸易与奴隶供给上相互冲突的地区势力
enemies.en: Its chief enemies were surrounding upland communities resisting its expansion and taxation, rival inland polities, and regional powers competing over trade and slave supply

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以巩固几内亚高地控制、维护伊斯兰权威、确保商路安全和通过有限军事行动压服边缘地区为主；尚未形成与欧洲国家直接平等外交的成熟制度
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on consolidating control of the Guinea highlands, upholding Islamic authority, securing trade routes, and subduing peripheral districts through limited military action; it had not yet developed a mature system of direct equal diplomacy with European states

era.zh: 启蒙时代时期的西非前近代高地国家
era.en: A West African premodern highland state in the Age of Enlightenment

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力不在机械创新，而在将阿拉伯文书、马立克派法学、古兰经教育网络、山地农业与骑兵政治组织结合；火器通过沿海贸易缓慢传入，但未引发全面军事革命
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay less in mechanical invention than in combining Arabic literacy, Maliki jurisprudence, Qur'anic schooling, highland agriculture, and cavalry-based political organization; firearms entered slowly through coastal trade but did not produce a full military revolution

strengths.zh: 国家的主要优势在于强烈的伊斯兰合法性、教士网络带来的组织能力、较稳固的山地防御环境、骑兵机动性以及连接内陆与沿海的区域贸易位置
strengths.en: The state's major strengths were its strong Islamic legitimacy, organizational capacity through clerical networks, relatively secure mountain defenses, cavalry mobility, and its regional position linking inland and coastal trade

weaknesses.zh: 弱点包括中央集权不足、派系竞争频繁、财政高度依赖实物与地方合作、火器与重型军备不足，以及对奴役劳动和外部贸易波动的依赖
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited centralization, frequent factional competition, heavy fiscal dependence on in-kind collection and local cooperation, insufficient firearms and heavy weaponry, and reliance on servile labor and unstable external trade

outlook.zh: 1750年的富塔贾隆总体稳定，短期内有能力维持神权秩序并扩展伊斯兰影响，但若内部派系失衡或沿海贸易与武器供给发生剧烈变化，其统一性与财政承受力将受到严峻考验
outlook.en: In 1750 Futa Jallon was broadly stable and capable in the short term of maintaining its theocratic order and extending Islamic influence, but severe internal factional imbalance or major shifts in coastal trade and arms supply would test its unity and fiscal resilience

description.zh: 几内亚高地的富拉尼伊斯兰神权国家。
description.en: A Fulani Islamic theocratic state in the Guinea highlands.

name.zh: 阿尔及尔以南撒哈拉商路诸政权
图阿雷格与萨赫勒贸易网络

ruler.zh: 诸阿蒙卡尔与商队首领

rulerTitle.zh: 阿蒙卡尔、部族首领与商队长
rulerTitle.en: Amenokal, tribal chief, and caravan leader

dynasty.zh: 诸部族世系与地方显贵家族
dynasty.en: Various tribal lineages and local notable families

capital.zh: 阿加德兹及其他绿洲中心

governmentForm.zh: 分散的部族邦联与绿洲城镇寡头治理相结合的商贸政治体系
governmentForm.en: A decentralized politico-commercial system combining tribal confederacies with oligarchic governance in oasis towns

socialStructure.zh: 社会由贵族战士阶层、宗教人士、商人、附庸群体、工匠与奴隶构成；城镇与游牧社会彼此依存，围绕盐、畜群、护路与跨撒哈拉贸易运转
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of noble warrior strata, religious specialists, merchants, dependent groups, artisans, and slaves; towns and nomadic communities were mutually dependent and organized around salt, livestock, route protection, and trans-Saharan trade

rulingClass.zh: 图阿雷格贵族氏族、阿加德兹等城镇商人寡头、伊斯兰学者与有影响力的商队家族
rulingClass.en: Tuareg noble clans, merchant oligarchs in towns such as Agadez, Islamic scholars, and influential caravan families

succession.zh: 部族首领多依习惯法与母系相关的贵族血统、氏族协商和武力威望推举；城镇权力则常在显贵家族与宗教精英间延续
succession.en: Succession among tribal leaders generally followed customary law, noble descent often linked through matrilineal prestige, clan consultation, and military reputation; urban authority often persisted within notable families and religious elites

structure.zh: 1750年前后的该体系并非统一国家，而是由阿伊尔、阿扎瓦格、塔德马克特边缘地带及若干萨赫勒联络点组成的松散网络。图阿雷格邦联负责沙漠道路安全、征收通行贡赋并仲裁部族事务；绿洲城镇则由苏丹、长官、商人议事群体与伊斯兰法学者共同管理
structure.en: Around 1750 this system was not a unitary state but a loose network spanning Air, Azawagh, the fringes of Tademekkat, and several Sahelian exchange nodes. Tuareg confederacies managed desert route security, levied transit dues, and arbitrated tribal affairs, while oasis towns were governed jointly by sultans, local officials, merchant councils, and Islamic jurists

function.zh: 组织商队通行、分配护卫、协调水源与宿营点、征收过路费并处理商旅纠纷
function.en: Organized caravan passage, assigned escorts, coordinated wells and encampments, collected transit tolls, and settled disputes among merchants and travelers

function.zh: 主持氏族协商、调停血仇、确认附庸关系、推举或承认地方首领
function.en: Oversaw clan consultation, mediated feuds, confirmed dependent relationships, and selected or recognized local chiefs

function.zh: 征收绿洲农产税、市场税、通行费、牲畜贡赋与保护费
function.en: Collected taxes on oasis agriculture, market dues, transit tolls, livestock tribute, and protection payments

function.zh: 依伊斯兰法与习惯法裁判婚姻、债务、贸易、继承与部族争端，并管理清真寺与学者网络
function.en: Judged marriage, debt, trade, inheritance, and tribal disputes under Islamic law and customary law, and supervised mosques and scholarly networks

function.zh: 维护井泉、椰枣园灌溉、粮盐仓储及城镇防御性物资储备
function.en: Maintained wells, date-palm irrigation, grain and salt storage, and defensive urban stockpiles

function.zh: 组织骑乘武装、保护商道、打击劫掠、回应邻近达尔富尔、豪萨与北非方向的威胁
function.en: Organized mounted armed forces, protected trade routes, suppressed raiding, and responded to threats from neighboring Darfur, Hausa regions, and North African directions

function.zh: 监督集市秩序、规范盐块、金粉、布匹与奴隶交易的计量标准
function.en: Supervised market order and standardized measurements for salt blocks, gold dust, textiles, and slave transactions

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由帐幕营地、氏族长老会、绿洲村社、城镇长官及伊斯兰法官共同承担。阿加德兹、因加勒、比尔马等中心各有不同治理传统，但都高度依赖商人、贵族与宗教精英的协商
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared among camp authorities, clan elder councils, oasis communities, town officials, and Islamic judges. Centers such as Agadez, In-Gall, and Bilma had varying traditions of rule, but all depended heavily on negotiation among merchants, nobles, and religious elites

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具马立克派伊斯兰法与图阿雷格习惯法。商贸、债务、婚姻、继承和契约常由卡迪或学者裁决；放牧权、通道权、复仇赔偿和部族从属则多按习惯法处理
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Maliki Islamic law with Tuareg customary law. Trade, debt, marriage, inheritance, and contracts were often judged by qadis or scholars, while grazing rights, passage rights, blood compensation, and tribal dependency were more often handled by custom

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要来自商道通行费、保护费、市场税、牲畜与绿洲农产征收、附庸群体贡赋，以及盐田和井泉使用相关收费。税率缺乏统一标准，常随季节、路线安全与首领权威而变化
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from route tolls, protection dues, market taxes, levies on livestock and oasis agriculture, tribute from dependent groups, and charges tied to salt works and wells. Rates were not uniformly standardized and often varied with season, route security, and the authority of individual chiefs

religion.zh: 主流宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教，法学上以马立克学派影响最深。伊斯兰信仰在城镇与商旅网络中根基牢固，但部分游牧地区仍保留较强的前伊斯兰习俗、护符文化与地方性仪式传统
religion.en: The dominant religion was Sunni Islam, with Maliki jurisprudence especially influential. Islam was deeply rooted in towns and trading networks, but some nomadic regions retained strong pre-Islamic customs, amuletic practices, and local ritual traditions

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以伊斯兰经学、法学、家谱记忆、口述诗歌和实用性的商旅伦理为核心，强调荣誉、款待、联盟义务、信义与在严酷环境中维持社会平衡
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Islamic scholarship, jurisprudence, genealogical memory, oral poetry, and a practical ethic of trade and travel, emphasizing honor, hospitality, alliance obligations, trust, and the maintenance of social balance in a harsh environment

writingSystem.zh: 书写主要使用阿拉伯字母记录阿拉伯语及阿贾米文本；图阿雷格社会也持续使用提非纳文字进行简短铭记、私人通信与身份标识
writingSystem.en: Writing primarily used the Arabic script for Arabic and Ajami texts; Tuareg society also continued to use Tifinagh for short inscriptions, private communication, and markers of identity

culturalAchievements.zh: 其文化成就在于维系跨撒哈拉交通体系、发展帐幕诗歌与音乐传统、保留提非纳文字、建立沙漠导引知识、培育伊斯兰学习中心，并在阿加德兹等地形成兼具萨赫勒与北非特色的建筑和手工艺风格
culturalAchievements.en: Its cultural achievements lay in maintaining trans-Saharan transport systems, developing tent poetry and music traditions, preserving Tifinagh, sustaining sophisticated desert navigation knowledge, nurturing Islamic learning centers, and shaping architectural and craft styles in places like Agadez that blended Sahelian and North African influences

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于柏柏尔语支中的图阿雷格语群，并广泛使用阿拉伯语作为宗教与书面交流语言；贸易中亦接触豪萨语、桑海语等萨赫勒语言
languageFamily.en: The principal languages belonged to the Tuareg branch of the Berber family, while Arabic was widely used for religion and written communication; trade also brought frequent contact with Hausa, Songhay, and other Sahelian languages

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模中等，核心优势不在高密度农业，而在于控制穿越中撒哈拉的运输、转口与安全服务。财富高度分散于畜群、盐、奴隶、布匹、债权和商队资本之中，现金化程度有限但商业流通能力很强
gdpDescription.en: The economy was moderate in overall size, with its principal strength lying not in dense agriculture but in the control of transport, brokerage, and security services across the central Sahara. Wealth was widely dispersed in herds, salt, slaves, textiles, credit, and caravan capital; monetization was limited, but commercial circulation was strong

mainIndustries.zh: 跨撒哈拉商队运输、盐矿开采与转运、畜牧业、椰枣与绿洲农业、皮革与金属手工业、奴隶贸易、地方集市中介与护路服务
mainIndustries.en: Trans-Saharan caravan transport, salt extraction and forwarding, pastoralism, date cultivation and oasis agriculture, leather and metal crafts, slave trading, local market brokerage, and route-protection services

tradeGoods.zh: 主要贸易品包括比尔马与塔乌代尼盐块、黄金与金粉、奴隶、骆驼、马匹、皮革、鸵鸟羽、象牙、椰枣、谷物，以及来自北方的布匹、铜器、武器、珠饰与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Main trade goods included salt blocks from Bilma and Taoudenni, gold and gold dust, slaves, camels, horses, leather, ostrich feathers, ivory, dates, grain, and northern imports such as textiles, copperware, weapons, beads, and luxury goods

name.zh: 多元货币与实物结算体系

unitName.zh: 米斯卡尔、玛丽亚·特蕾莎银元及贝壳/盐块折算
unitName.en: mithqal, silver dollars, and cowrie/salt equivalents

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。游牧贵族与大商人往往拥有大量骆驼、牛羊、奴隶与债权；普通牧民和绿洲农户则财富有限，易受干旱、劫掠和商路波动影响
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously. Nomadic nobles and major merchants often controlled substantial camel herds, livestock, slaves, and credit claims, while ordinary herders and oasis cultivators possessed far less and were vulnerable to drought, raiding, and fluctuations in caravan traffic

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系属于商队资本、部族保护、绿洲农业和附庸劳动并存的混合型区域经济。其运行依赖信用、人身依附、保护契约和跨文化中介，而非统一国家市场
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a mixed regional economy in which caravan capital, tribal protection, oasis agriculture, and dependent labor coexisted. It functioned through credit, personal dependency, protection contracts, and intercultural brokerage rather than a unified state market

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

source.zh: 牲畜、绿洲农产与井泉相关税赋
source.en: Levies on livestock, oasis produce, and wells

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

source.en: Tribute from dependent groups and shares of spoils

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

category.zh: 地方首领、盟约与贡礼分配
category.en: Distribution to chiefs, alliances, and tribute gifts

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

category.en: Maintenance of wells, irrigation, and storage

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

category.zh: 城镇建筑与礼仪性支出

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔
西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

treasuryDescription.zh: 所谓"国库"并不集中于单一宫廷，而是分散于阿加德兹等城镇库藏、首领私库、盐块储备、牲畜财富与商队货物中。流动性强，但也更易受劫掠与旱灾影响
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' was not concentrated in a single court but dispersed among urban storehouses in places like Agadez, chiefs' private reserves, salt stocks, livestock wealth, and caravan goods. This gave it flexibility, but also made it more exposed to raiding and drought

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持商路畅通、确保绿洲供给、通过礼物和分配巩固同盟，并避免过度榨取商人以免改道。稳定优先于官僚化集中
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized keeping caravan routes open, securing oasis supplies, strengthening alliances through gifts and redistribution, and avoiding excessive burdens on merchants that might divert trade elsewhere. Stability was valued over bureaucratic centralization

description.zh: 负责长距离侦察、护送商队、守卫井泉与追击沙漠劫掠者，是最具代表性的机动力量
description.en: Responsible for long-range reconnaissance, caravan escort, guarding wells, and pursuing desert raiders; they were the most characteristic mobile force

description.zh: 主要用于突击、部族冲突和对萨赫勒边缘地带的快速行动，装备长矛、刀剑和部分火器
description.en: Used mainly for shock action, tribal conflict, and rapid operations on the Sahelian margins, equipped with spears, swords, and some firearms

description.zh: 驻守阿加德兹、比尔马等节点，守卫粮仓、城墙、盐仓和集市，也在商队中承担近距离护卫任务
description.en: Stationed in nodes such as Agadez and Bilma to defend granaries, walls, salt depots, and markets, and also served as close caravan guards

description.zh: 在商路危机、血仇升级或外敌压境时临时征集，战斗力差异较大
description.en: Mobilized temporarily during route crises, escalating feuds, or external threats, with highly variable combat effectiveness

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一最高统帅，由各邦联阿蒙卡尔与主要商队首领分掌
commanderInChief.en: No single supreme commander; authority was divided among confederation amenokals and major caravan leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以适应沙漠环境为要。武器包括长矛、剑、匕首、盾牌和数量有限的火绳枪、燧发枪；真正的优势在于骑乘能力、路线知识、补水管理与长距离机动，而非重炮或正规步兵操典
technology.en: Military technology was defined above all by adaptation to the desert environment. Weapons included spears, swords, daggers, shields, and limited numbers of matchlocks and flintlocks; the real advantages lay in riding skill, route knowledge, water management, and long-range mobility rather than artillery or formal infantry drill

unit.zh: 西非法郎贝壳折算利弗尔

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自商道劫掠、部族内斗、豪萨与萨赫勒政治格局变化、达尔富尔和北非方向对贸易通道的竞争，以及旱灾造成的资源冲突
threats.en: Main threats came from caravan raiding, intertribal conflict, shifting Hausa and Sahelian political conditions, competition over trade routes from Darfur and North African directions, and resource conflicts caused by drought

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏而流动性高，集中分布于阿伊尔山地、阿加德兹及其周边绿洲、盐矿地带和南缘萨赫勒集市。多数人口从事游牧、半游牧、绿洲农业和商队相关活动
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and highly mobile, concentrated in the Air massif, Agadez and surrounding oases, salt-producing zones, and Sahelian market fringes. Most people engaged in pastoralism, semi-nomadic life, oasis cultivation, and caravan-related work

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致包括贵族战士、宗教学者、自由平民牧民与农民、商人、工匠、附庸群体及奴隶。不同邦联内部结构并不完全相同，但等级性和依附关系普遍存在
socialClasses.en: Social strata broadly included noble warriors, religious scholars, free commoner herders and cultivators, merchants, artisans, dependent groups, and slaves. Internal structures varied across confederacies, but hierarchy and dependent relationships were widespread

allies.zh: 通常与阿伊尔周边绿洲城镇、部分豪萨商邦、达尔富尔商人、北非中介商及撒哈拉沿线宗教网络保持务实合作；联盟高度地方化且随商路利益变动
allies.en: They typically maintained pragmatic cooperation with oasis towns around Air, certain Hausa trading states, Darfur merchants, North African intermediaries, and religious networks along the Sahara; alliances were highly localized and shifted with route interests

enemies.zh: 没有固定统一的外敌，但常与竞争商路的部族联盟、劫掠集团、试图绕开其控制的邻近政权，以及内部敌对氏族发生冲突
enemies.en: There was no single fixed external enemy, but conflict was frequent with rival tribal alliances controlling competing routes, raiding groups, neighboring powers attempting to bypass their influence, and hostile internal clans

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持商路、平衡北非与萨赫勒贸易方向、通过婚姻与贡礼稳固联盟、并在必要时以武力确保道路与井泉控制为核心。其外交更像网络协调而非国家间常设外交
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maintaining caravan routes, balancing North African and Sahelian trade directions, securing alliances through marriage and gifts, and using force when necessary to retain control over roads and wells. Its diplomacy was more a matter of network coordination than permanent interstate relations

era.zh: 近世早期撒哈拉—萨赫勒商队时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与知识包括成熟的沙漠导航、井泉与绿洲管理、驮运骆驼编组、盐矿开采和长距离商队后勤。尽管火器逐渐进入该地区，但并未像沿海国家那样引发全面军事革命
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and knowledge included advanced desert navigation, well and oasis management, organized camel transport, salt-mining methods, and long-distance caravan logistics. Although firearms were gradually entering the region, they had not produced a full military revolution comparable to that of major coastal states

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于掌握撒哈拉腹地交通命脉、卓越的沙漠适应能力、跨文化商业中介地位以及灵活的部族联盟机制。即使缺乏集中官僚国家，也能维系广域贸易与地方秩序
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths lay in controlling vital interior Saharan routes, possessing exceptional desert adaptation, occupying a cross-cultural brokerage role, and relying on flexible tribal alliance mechanisms. Even without a centralized bureaucratic state, it could sustain wide-ranging trade and local order

weaknesses.zh: 主要弱点是政治碎片化、财政分散、对降雨与井泉极度依赖、城镇生产基础薄弱，以及商路一旦转移便会迅速削弱整体收入。内部等级冲突与奴役制度也限制了长期整合能力
weaknesses.en: Its main weaknesses were political fragmentation, dispersed finance, acute dependence on rainfall and wells, a weak urban production base, and the ease with which overall income could decline if trade routes shifted. Internal hierarchies and slave dependence also limited long-term integration

outlook.zh: 1750年前后前景总体稳定，但中长期将面临更大压力：北非与大西洋沿岸贸易重心变化、萨赫勒国家兴衰、火器扩散以及未来欧洲势力向内陆推进，都可能削弱传统跨撒哈拉贸易网络的主导地位
outlook.en: Around 1750 the outlook was broadly stable, but the medium- to long-term picture carried mounting pressures: changes in the balance between North African and Atlantic commerce, the rise and fall of Sahelian states, the spread of firearms, and future European penetration inland could all weaken the dominant position of the traditional trans-Saharan trade networks

description.zh: 由图阿雷格联盟、绿洲城镇与跨撒哈拉商路构成的广域政治经济体系。
description.en: A broad politico-economic system made up of Tuareg confederacies, oasis towns, and trans-Saharan caravan routes.

name.zh: 尤卡坦与危地马拉高地的原住民政体
name.en: Indigenous Polities of Yucatan and the Guatemalan Highlands

ruler.zh: 诸地方首领、村社长老与世袭本地统治者
ruler.en: Various local rulers, chiefs, elders, and hereditary local authorities

dynasty.en: Local lineages and regional hereditary houses

capital.zh: 诸地方中心，如马尼、索托塔、莫莫斯特南戈、奇奇卡斯特南戈与托托尼卡潘等
capital.en: Various local centers, such as Mani, Sotuta, Momostenango, Chichicastenango, and Totonicapan

governmentForm.zh: 殖民统治下保留自治空间的村社—部族混合政体
governmentForm.en: Village-tribal polities retaining limited autonomy under colonial rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会以村社、亲族、教区与族群共同体为核心，上层为本地贵族与卡西克家族，中层为手工业者、商贩与村社官员，下层为广大农民、佃作者与服役劳工。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on villages, kin groups, parishes, and ethnic communities, with local nobles and cacique families at the top, artisans, traders, and village officeholders in the middle, and the broad mass of farmers, tenants, and laborers below.

rulingClass.zh: 本地贵族家族、卡西克、长老议事群体、村社官员与与殖民政权合作的土著中介者
rulingClass.en: Local noble lineages, caciques, councils of elders, village officers, and indigenous intermediaries who dealt with colonial authorities

succession.zh: 以地方宗族世袭为主，辅以长老认可、村社协商及殖民官府确认
succession.en: Primarily hereditary within local lineages, supplemented by elder approval, community consensus, and confirmation by colonial authorities

structure.zh: 政治结构高度分散。各玛雅社区通常由村社长老、本地贵族、教区官员与西班牙殖民体制承认的卡比尔多印第奥共同治理；在高地地区，旧有族群领地与镇社共和国形式并存。
structure.en: The political structure was highly decentralized. Maya communities were generally governed by village elders, local nobles, parish officers, and cabildos de indios recognized by the Spanish colonial system; in the highlands, older ethnic territories coexisted with town republic institutions.

function.zh: 处理土地分配、劳役协调、地方纠纷、节庆组织与对上级官府的代表事务
function.en: Handled land allocation, labor coordination, local disputes, festival organization, and representation before higher authorities

function.zh: 在殖民法律框架下管理镇社行政、贡赋登记、治安与文书事务
function.en: Managed town administration, tribute registration, policing, and documentation within the colonial legal framework

function.zh: 组织守护圣人庆典、教会捐献、兄弟会活动，并维系天主教与本地仪式传统的结合
function.en: Organized patron saint festivals, church contributions, confraternity activities, and maintained the blending of Catholic and local ritual traditions

function.zh: 统计人口、征收贡赋、安排运输、募集公共工程劳役与向殖民当局报告
function.en: Counted population, collected tribute, organized transport, mobilized labor for public works, and reported to colonial authorities

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以印第安人镇社、教区与传统村社边界为基础。尤卡坦地区常见受殖民监督的本地首领体系；危地马拉高地则保留更多族群共同体、长老和宗教职务网络。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on Indian towns, parishes, and traditional village boundaries. In Yucatan, local chiefly structures operated under colonial supervision; in the Guatemalan highlands, stronger ethnic communities, elder authority, and religious office networks persisted.

legalSystem.zh: 法律实践为西班牙殖民法、教会法与习惯法并行。婚姻、土地、贡赋和继承等事务常由本地官员与神职人员调解，重大案件则上呈殖民法庭。
legalSystem.en: Legal practice combined Spanish colonial law, canon law, and customary law. Matters of marriage, land, tribute, and inheritance were often mediated by local officials and clergy, while major cases were referred to colonial courts.

taxationSystem.zh: 税负主要表现为人头贡、实物贡、教会什一相关负担、运输与劳役义务。许多社区通过集体承担方式分配税负，以减轻个体风险。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation fell mainly in the form of head tribute, in-kind tribute, church-related dues, transport duties, and labor obligations. Many communities distributed these burdens collectively to reduce individual risk.

religion.zh: 以罗马天主教为官方宗教，但广泛与玛雅传统宇宙观、祖灵崇敬、圣山圣泉崇拜和历法仪式融合
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the official religion, but it was widely fused with Maya cosmology, ancestor reverence, sacred mountain and spring cults, and calendrical ritual traditions

philosophy.zh: 思想核心重视共同体义务、祖先合法性、土地与神圣地景的联系，以及天主教道德与玛雅礼仪知识的并存
philosophy.en: The intellectual core emphasized communal obligation, ancestral legitimacy, the link between land and sacred landscape, and the coexistence of Catholic morality with Maya ritual knowledge

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母记录尤卡坦玛雅语、基切语、卡克奇克尔语等语言；传统象形文字系统在公共行政中已不再使用，但古代文本记忆仍通过口传与殖民时代文献延续
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was chiefly used to write Yucatec Maya, K'iche', Kaqchikel, and related languages; the classical hieroglyphic system was no longer used in public administration, but memory of ancient texts survived through oral tradition and colonial-era manuscripts

culturalAchievements.zh: 保存了丰富的编织、陶艺、木雕、仪式舞蹈、宗教戏剧、地方编年记录和玛雅语言文学传统；高地地区持续发展华丽纺织图案与兄弟会节庆文化。
culturalAchievements.en: They preserved rich traditions of weaving, pottery, wood carving, ritual dance, religious drama, local annals, and Maya-language literary culture; the highlands continued to develop elaborate textile designs and confraternity festival culture.

languageFamily.zh: 玛雅语系
languageFamily.en: Mayan language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业为主，辅以地方市场交换、手工业和向殖民城市供应粮食、纺织品、染料与劳务。人均产出不高，但社区维持能力较强。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was dominated by subsistence agriculture, supplemented by local market exchange, handicrafts, and the supply of foodstuffs, textiles, dyes, and labor to colonial towns. Output per capita was modest, but community resilience was relatively strong.

mainIndustries.zh: 玉米、豆类与南瓜农业，棉纺织，龙舌兰与绳索制作，胭脂虫与染料生产，陶器，家畜饲养，运输与地方贸易
mainIndustries.en: Maize, bean, and squash agriculture, cotton weaving, agave and cordage production, cochineal and dye production, pottery, livestock raising, transport, and local trade

tradeGoods.zh: 玉米、豆类、辣椒、棉布、羊毛织物、陶器、木材、盐、蜂蜜、蜡、胭脂虫、靛蓝、小牲畜及地方手工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Maize, beans, chili peppers, cotton cloth, woolen textiles, pottery, timber, salt, honey, wax, cochineal, indigo, small livestock, and local handicrafts

name.zh: 西班牙比索与地方实物交换并用
name.en: Spanish peso alongside local barter exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在耕地使用权、玉米储备、织机、家畜、果树、住房和宗教职务声望，而非大量货币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly in access to cultivated land, maize stores, looms, livestock, fruit trees, housing, and prestige through religious office, rather than in large monetary reserves.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社土地制度和家庭农业为基础，并嵌入西班牙殖民贡赋市场体系的混合经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on communal landholding and household agriculture, embedded within the Spanish colonial tribute and market system

source.zh: 宗教兄弟会与特别捐输

category.zh: 向殖民政权缴纳贡赋与法定负担
category.en: Tribute and statutory payments to the colonial regime

category.zh: 宗教节庆、兄弟会与教区开支
category.en: Religious festivals, confraternities, and parish expenses
Public works, roads, and water management

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无统一国家金库，资金分散保存在各镇社公款、兄弟会基金、教区箱柜与地方首领掌握的粮食和实物储备中。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no single state treasury; funds were dispersed among town-community monies, confraternity funds, parish chests, and grain and material reserves held by local leaders.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持贡赋履行、社区再分配与宗教公共生活；在歉收年份常以延期、集体分摊或出售公产应急。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized tribute compliance, community redistribution, and religious public life; in poor harvest years communities often resorted to delays, collective burden-sharing, or sales of common assets.

description.zh: 由村社成年男子组成，平时务农，战时或治安需要时集结，主要用于守卫道路、追捕盗匪和社区防卫。
description.en: Composed of adult male villagers who farmed in peacetime and assembled for war or security needs, mainly to guard roads, pursue bandits, and defend communities.

description.zh: 负责集镇巡逻、押运贡赋、保护仓储和协助殖民地方官维持秩序。
description.en: Responsible for patrolling towns, escorting tribute, protecting storehouses, and assisting colonial local authorities in maintaining order.

description.zh: 由较富裕家族及首领亲信构成，装备较好，在冲突中担任骨干与指挥传达角色。
description.en: Drawn from wealthier families and chiefly retainers, they were somewhat better equipped and served as cadres and messengers in conflicts.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各地首领、卡西克与殖民认可的地方官分别指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; command was exercised separately by local chiefs, caciques, and colonial-recognized local officers

technology.zh: 武备以火绳枪、猎枪、刀矛、弓箭、木盾与简易防御工事为主，武器质量和火药供应依赖地方条件及殖民市场。
technology.en: Armament centered on matchlocks, hunting guns, blades, spears, bows, wooden shields, and simple fieldworks, with weapon quality and gunpowder supply depending on local conditions and colonial markets.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括地方盗匪、边远地区叛乱风险、殖民征发冲突、土地兼并压力，以及加勒比沿岸与边缘地带的外来袭扰。
threats.en: Main threats included local banditry, the risk of unrest in remote areas, conflicts arising from colonial requisitions, pressure from land encroachment, and occasional external raiding along Caribbean and frontier zones.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要由尤卡坦玛雅人、基切人、卡克奇克尔人、马姆人及其他玛雅语族群构成，另有少量混血、非洲裔与西班牙人居于城镇和行政节点。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted chiefly of Yucatec Maya, K'iche', Kaqchikel, Mam, and other Maya-speaking peoples, with smaller mestizo, African-descended, and Spanish populations in towns and administrative centers.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括本地贵族与卡西克家族、村社官员、宗教职务承担者、手工业者和商贩、普通自耕农、佃作与劳役人口；阶层差异存在，但共同体身份通常强于严格的国家式等级制。
socialClasses.en: Social strata included local noble and cacique families, village officers, holders of religious offices, artisans and traders, ordinary peasant cultivators, and tenant or laboring populations; distinctions existed, but communal identity was often stronger than a rigid state-like hierarchy.

allies.zh: 无统一对外盟友；实际关系主要是与西属新西班牙殖民当局、地方教会机构及邻近原住民共同体维持协商与互助
allies.en: There were no unified external allies; practical relations centered on negotiation and mutual support with the colonial authorities of Spanish New Spain, local church institutions, and neighboring indigenous communities

enemies.zh: 无单一国家级敌人，但与苛重贡赋的殖民官员、侵占土地的地主、地方匪帮及敌对邻村时有冲突
enemies.en: There was no single state-level enemy, but conflicts arose with burdensome colonial officials, land-grabbing estate owners, local bandits, and rival neighboring communities

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存、防卫与地方自治保存为核心，通过请愿、诉讼、妥协、宗教合作和有限武装抵抗维护土地、贡赋减免与传统权利。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on survival, defense, and the preservation of local autonomy, using petitions, litigation, compromise, religious cooperation, and limited armed resistance to defend land, tribute relief, and traditional rights.

era.zh: 启蒙时代中的殖民边缘地方社会
era.en: A colonial frontier society within the Age of Enlightenment

keyInnovations.zh: 采用拉丁字母文书、教会音乐与建筑形式、欧洲式铁器工具、畜力运输和火器；同时延续高效山地梯田、地方灌溉、染织技术与草药知识。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included alphabetic record keeping, church music and architectural forms, European iron tools, animal-powered transport, and firearms; at the same time they retained effective terrace farming, local irrigation, dyeing and weaving techniques, and herbal knowledge.

strengths.zh: 优势在于强韧的村社组织、深厚的土地与亲族联系、较高的文化延续性、成熟的地方市场网络，以及在殖民秩序内外灵活周旋的能力。
strengths.en: Strengths included resilient village organization, deep ties of land and kinship, strong cultural continuity, established local market networks, and the ability to maneuver flexibly both within and outside the colonial order.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于缺乏统一政权、军事力量分散、长期承受贡赋与劳役压力、易受疫病与歉收影响，并持续面对土地被侵夺和行政干预。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses lay in the lack of a unified state, fragmented military power, long-term pressure from tribute and labor demands, vulnerability to disease and harvest failure, and continued exposure to land seizure and administrative interference.

outlook.zh: 1750年前后总体保持稳定，但这种稳定建立在地方妥协之上。若殖民改革加强税负、土地私有化和行政集中化，未来数十年紧张局势与地方反抗很可能上升。
outlook.en: Around 1750 the region was broadly stable, but this stability rested on local compromise. If colonial reforms intensified taxation, land privatization, and administrative centralization, tensions and local resistance were likely to grow in the coming decades.

description.zh: 尽管处于殖民框架之下，玛雅地区仍保留众多原住民共同体与地方权威结构。
description.en: Despite colonial rule, the Maya world still retained numerous Indigenous communities and local authority structures.

name.zh: 西班牙新西班牙总督区

ruler.zh: 胡安·弗朗西斯科·德·古埃梅斯-奥尔卡西塔斯
ruler.en: Juan Francisco de Guemes y Horcasitas

governmentForm.zh: 波旁西班牙君主制之下的总督殖民行政区，由总督、审问院、总司库与教会体系共同治理
governmentForm.en: A viceregal colonial administration under the Bourbon Spanish monarchy, governed through the viceroy, audiencias, treasury offices, and the ecclesiastical hierarchy

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格等级化，顶层为半岛出生的西班牙人，其次为美洲出生的克里奥尔人，再下为梅斯蒂索、印第安共和国居民、非洲裔自由民与奴隶，以及各种混血法定类别
socialStructure.en: Society was strongly stratified, with peninsular Spaniards at the top, followed by American-born creoles, then mestizos, residents of the Indian republics, free people of African descent and slaves, alongside numerous legally recognized mixed-status groups

rulingClass.zh: 总督府高官、审问院法官、皇家财政官员、主教团、半岛出生商人与矿业精英，以及部分大地产克里奥尔家族
rulingClass.en: Viceregal high officials, audiencia magistrates, royal treasury officers, the episcopate, peninsular merchant and mining elites, and some great creole landed families

succession.zh: 总督由西班牙国王任命，属非世袭殖民官职；王权更替依西班牙波旁王朝继承法办理
succession.en: The viceroy was appointed by the King of Spain and was not hereditary; dynastic succession followed the succession rules of the Spanish Bourbon monarchy

structure.zh: 1750年的新西班牙由总督统辖，首府设于墨西哥城，下辖墨西哥审问院、瓜达拉哈拉审问院、各省长官区、市镇议会、王室财政机构、军区与教会司法体系；波旁改革已加强王权对税收、军务与贸易的直接控制
structure.en: In 1750 New Spain was governed by the viceroy from Mexico City, under which stood the Audiencia of Mexico, the Audiencia of Guadalajara, provincial governorships, municipal cabildos, royal treasury offices, military districts, and ecclesiastical jurisdictions; Bourbon reforms had already begun to strengthen direct royal control over revenue, military affairs, and commerce

function.zh: 协调总督命令、殖民行政、军政调度、与西班牙王廷及印度事务委员会往来
function.en: Coordinated viceregal orders, colonial administration, military-political management, and correspondence with the Spanish court and the Council of the Indies

function.zh: 征收阿尔卡瓦拉销售税、贡赋、采矿税、烟酒专卖收入、海关税并管理国库
function.en: Collected alcabala sales tax, tribute, mining dues, monopoly revenues, customs, and managed the royal treasury

function.zh: 负责高级司法、行政监督、对地方官申诉与在总督空缺时执行临时治理
function.en: Handled high justice, administrative oversight, appeals from local officials, and interim governance when the viceroyalty lacked a sitting viceroy

function.zh: 管理省长辖区、市镇议会、印第安共和国村社、治安、劳役分配与地方税收记录
function.en: Managed provincial governorships, municipal cabildos, Indian republic communities, policing, labor obligations, and local tax records

function.zh: 组织驻军、民兵、边境总统辖区、防御工事、海岸巡防与北方边疆防务
function.en: Organized garrisons, militias, frontier presidios, fortifications, coastal patrols, and northern border defense

name.zh: 教会与王室保教权事务

function.zh: 协调主教区、修会、什一税相关事务、教区编制、传教站与王室保教权下的教职任命程序
function.en: Coordinated dioceses, religious orders, tithe-related matters, parish organization, missions, and ecclesiastical appointments under royal patronage

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依靠省长、总督辖下军政长官、科雷希多尔、市镇议会与印第安共和国首领。乡村印第安社群保留一定自治传统，但受王室司法、神职人员和税务官监督。
localAdmin.en: Local administration depended on governors, subordinate military-political officers, corregidores, municipal cabildos, and leaders of the Indian republics. Rural indigenous communities retained limited customary self-government but were supervised by royal judges, clergy, and tax officials.

legalSystem.zh: 法律基础为《卡斯蒂利亚法》、印度法及王室敕令，辅以地方惯例和教会法。司法由审问院、地方法官、教会法庭与军事司法并行。
legalSystem.en: The legal order rested on Castilian law, the Laws of the Indies, and royal decrees, supplemented by local custom and canon law. Justice was administered through audiencias, local magistrates, ecclesiastical courts, and military jurisdiction.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以印第安贡赋、阿尔卡瓦拉销售税、关税、采矿税、汞与火药等专卖和国家垄断收入、印花税及教会什一税相关分流构成；税收征解层级复杂，承包征收与官收并存。
taxationSystem.en: The tax system was built on indigenous tribute, the alcabala sales tax, customs duties, mining dues, monopoly and state-controlled revenues from items such as mercury and gunpowder, stamped paper fees, and fiscal diversions connected to the tithe; collection was multilayered and combined tax farming with direct royal administration.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教与社会核心，方济各会、多明我会、奥古斯丁会与耶稣会在教育、传教与地方社会中地位突出，同时存在广泛的本土宗教习俗与天主教混融
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the established religion and social core, with Franciscans, Dominicans, Augustinians, and Jesuits playing major roles in education, missions, and local society, while extensive indigenous religious practices persisted in syncretic form

philosophy.zh: 思想上以天主教经院传统、王权合法性、晚期巴洛克敬虔文化为主，同时受启蒙时代自然法、实用科学与波旁改革理性行政观念有限影响
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was dominated by Catholic scholasticism, royalist legitimacy, and late Baroque devotional culture, though it was increasingly touched by Enlightenment natural law, practical science, and the rational-administrative ideas associated with Bourbon reform

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为西班牙语拉丁字母文书；教会与学术文献大量使用拉丁文；部分原住民语言以拉丁字母拼写用于教理、行政与地方记录
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was Spanish in the Latin alphabet; Latin remained important in church and scholarship; some indigenous languages were also written in Latin script for catechisms, administration, and local records

culturalAchievements.zh: 新西班牙在巴洛克建筑、宗教绘画、银器工艺、大学教育、地图测绘与跨太平洋文化交流方面成就显著。墨西哥城、普埃布拉与瓦哈卡拥有高度发展的教堂建筑与手工艺传统，印第安与西班牙艺术形式深度融合。
culturalAchievements.en: New Spain achieved distinction in Baroque architecture, religious painting, silverwork, university education, cartography, and trans-Pacific cultural exchange. Mexico City, Puebla, and Oaxaca sustained highly developed church architecture and artisanal traditions, with deep blending of indigenous and Spanish artistic forms.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与上层通用语言属罗曼语族伊比利亚分支的西班牙语；境内广泛使用纳瓦特尔语、玛雅诸语、米斯特克语、萨波特克语、奥托米语等多种美洲原住民语言
languageFamily.en: The official and elite language was Spanish, an Iberian Romance language; the territory also widely used Nahuatl, Mayan languages, Mixtec, Zapotec, Otomi, and many other indigenous American language families

gdpDescription.zh: 新西班牙是18世纪中叶西班牙帝国最富庶的殖民辖区之一，经济以白银开采、农业、区域市场和大规模跨洋贸易为支柱。其人均产出高于许多美洲地区，但财富分配极不均衡。
gdpDescription.en: New Spain was one of the wealthiest colonial jurisdictions of the mid-eighteenth-century Spanish Empire, with silver mining, agriculture, regional markets, and large-scale oceanic trade as its main pillars. Output per head was high by many American standards, but wealth was distributed very unevenly.

mainIndustries.zh: 白银与金矿开采、汞法炼银、牧畜业、玉米与小麦农业、甘蔗种植、纺织作坊、染料生产、皮革、烟草加工、航运补给与城市手工业
mainIndustries.en: Silver and gold mining, mercury-based silver refining, livestock raising, maize and wheat agriculture, sugar cultivation, textile workshops, dye production, leatherworking, tobacco processing, shipping supply, and urban crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 白银、胭脂虫红、靛蓝、糖、可可、皮革、香草、烟草、纺织品、瓷器转口品、丝绸与来自马尼拉大帆船贸易的亚洲奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Silver, cochineal dye, indigo, sugar, cacao, hides, vanilla, tobacco, textiles, re-exported porcelain, silk, and Asian luxury goods arriving through the Manila galleon trade

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。矿主、商人和大庄园主拥有大量地产、牲畜、信贷网络与银锭；城市中产手工业者与店主维持稳定收入；多数印第安农户、小佃农与混血劳工资产有限，常依赖社区土地、季节性工资与债务关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously. Mine owners, merchants, and great estate holders possessed substantial land, livestock, credit networks, and bullion; urban middling artisans and shopkeepers maintained stable incomes; most indigenous cultivators, small tenants, and mixed-race laborers held limited assets and often relied on communal land, seasonal wages, and debt ties.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为受重商主义约束的殖民市场经济，由王室垄断、特许贸易、矿业资本、教会资金、地方市场和强制性税赋共同构成。对外贸易原则上受塞维利亚—加的斯体系与帝国内部航线规范约束，但走私长期存在。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a colonial market economy constrained by mercantilism, shaped by royal monopolies, licensed trade, mining capital, ecclesiastical finance, local markets, and coercive taxation. Overseas commerce was in principle regulated by the Seville-Cadiz imperial system and intra-imperial shipping rules, though smuggling remained chronic.

source.zh: 采矿相关税与铸币收入
印花税、司法费用与杂项
source.en: Stamped paper, judicial fees, and miscellaneous receipts

category.zh: 海军补给、港口与舰队协防
category.en: Naval supply, ports, and fleet support

category.zh: 对其他殖民地与王室汇拨
category.en: Transfers to other colonies and the Crown

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以墨西哥城皇家金库与若干地区金库为核心，储备丰厚但经常用于军费、舰队、边疆驻防和向加勒比与菲律宾转移银款。账面结余虽强，但运输风险、战争需求与王室临时征调会迅速消耗库存。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury centered on the royal coffers of Mexico City and several regional treasuries, and was substantial but frequently tapped for military costs, fleets, frontier presidios, and silver remittances to the Caribbean and the Philippines. Although strong on paper, reserves could be quickly drained by transport risks, wartime needs, and extraordinary royal demands.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体偏保守且重商主义，核心目标是保证白银生产、维持殖民秩序、保障跨洋转运并为西班牙王室提供稳定现金流。波旁改革正在推动更严密审计与更高效征收。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative and mercantilist, aiming above all to preserve silver production, maintain colonial order, secure transoceanic remittances, and furnish the Spanish Crown with dependable cash flow. Bourbon reforms were pushing toward tighter auditing and more efficient collection.

description.zh: 由驻墨西哥城、韦拉克鲁斯、哈瓦那联系线及主要省份的王室步兵团组成，承担守备、镇压骚乱与港口防御任务
description.en: Consisted of royal infantry regiments stationed in Mexico City, along the Veracruz-Havana line of communication, and in major provinces, serving in garrison duty, riot suppression, and port defense

description.zh: 活跃于北方边疆、内陆交通线和大庄园区，用于快速巡逻、护送与对抗游动边境部族
description.en: Active on the northern frontier, interior communications routes, and estate zones, they provided rapid patrols, escorts, and responses against mobile borderland raiding groups

description.zh: 分布于新比斯开亚、索诺拉、得克萨斯与新墨西哥等边境据点，兼具军事驻防与殖民前哨功能
description.en: Stationed across frontier strongpoints in Nueva Vizcaya, Sonora, Texas, and New Mexico, they combined military garrison duties with colonial outpost functions

description.zh: 由城市居民、商人、种植园主、自由有色民与地方精英组织，战时用于补充守军、海岸防御和地方治安
description.en: Organized from urban residents, merchants, estate owners, free people of color, and local elites, they were mobilized in wartime for reinforcement, coastal defense, and local security

name.zh: 海岸炮兵与海军辅助力量
name.en: Coastal Artillery and Naval Auxiliary Forces

description.zh: 负责圣胡安-乌卢阿等炮台、军港与护航补给，虽非独立大型海军，但对加勒比和墨西哥湾防务重要
description.en: Manned fortresses such as San Juan de Ulua, ports, and convoy support systems; though not an independent major navy, they were important for Caribbean and Gulf defense

commanderInChief.zh: 新西班牙总督兼总队长
commanderInChief.en: The Viceroy of New Spain as Captain General

technology.zh: 军备处于18世纪中叶欧洲殖民标准，使用燧发枪、火炮、要塞炮台、骑兵马刀与线列步兵战术；边疆战争则更多依赖轻骑巡逻、侦察与驻防网络。
technology.en: Military technology matched mid-eighteenth-century European colonial standards, using flintlock muskets, artillery, fortified batteries, cavalry sabers, and line infantry tactics; frontier warfare relied more on light mounted patrols, scouting, and garrison networks.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自英国海军与私掠、加勒比海竞争、北方边疆阿帕奇与科曼奇等游动势力袭扰、法国与英国在北美的地缘竞争，以及走私和地方叛乱风险。
threats.en: Principal threats came from British naval power and privateering, Caribbean rivalry, raids by mobile northern frontier groups such as Apaches and Comanches, French and British geopolitical competition in North America, and the risks of smuggling and local unrest.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约六百万，是西班牙美洲人口最稠密的地区之一。人口主要集中于墨西哥高原、中部谷地、普埃布拉、瓦哈卡、瓜达拉哈拉地区及若干矿业中心，北方边疆则稀疏分散。
populationDescription.en: With about six million inhabitants, New Spain was one of the most populous parts of Spanish America. Population was concentrated in the Mexican plateau, central valleys, Puebla, Oaxaca, the Guadalajara region, and several mining centers, while the northern frontier remained thinly settled.

socialClasses.zh: 人口构成为原住民多数，其次为梅斯蒂索、克里奥尔与半岛西班牙人，以及非洲裔和复杂混血群体。社会地位、税负、法律待遇与职业机会与种族分类、地产占有和教会联系紧密相关。
socialClasses.en: The population consisted of an indigenous majority, followed by mestizos, creoles and peninsular Spaniards, along with people of African descent and many mixed groups. Social status, tax burdens, legal treatment, and occupational opportunities were closely tied to racial classification, landholding, and ties to the Church.

allies.zh: 名义盟友为西班牙帝国其他辖区与波旁西班牙本土；在欧洲外交框架中与法国波旁王朝保持家族协作倾向，但在美洲主要依赖帝国内部协调而非独立外交
allies.en: Its nominal allies were the other jurisdictions of the Spanish Empire and Bourbon Spain itself; within the wider European framework it inclined toward dynastic cooperation with Bourbon France, though in the Americas it relied chiefly on intra-imperial coordination rather than independent diplomacy

enemies.zh: 主要对手为大不列颠及其海军力量、加勒比私掠网络，以及北美边疆上与西班牙殖民推进冲突的若干原住民军事联盟；与葡萄牙巴西也存在边界与走私摩擦
enemies.en: Its principal adversaries were Great Britain and its naval power, privateering networks in the Caribbean, and several indigenous military coalitions resisting Spanish expansion on the North American frontier; there were also border and smuggling frictions with Portuguese Brazil

foreignPolicy.zh: 新西班牙并无完全独立外交，其对外政策服务于西班牙帝国战略：保护白银航线、防御加勒比与墨西哥湾、限制外国走私、稳固北方边疆、维持与菲律宾及秘鲁间的帝国联系。
foreignPolicy.en: New Spain did not possess fully independent diplomacy; its external policy served Spanish imperial strategy: protecting silver routes, defending the Caribbean and Gulf of Mexico, restricting foreign smuggling, securing the northern frontier, and maintaining imperial links with the Philippines and Peru.

era.zh: 启蒙时代早期近代殖民技术体系
era.en: Early modern colonial technology in the Age of Enlightenment

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括成熟的汞法炼银、规模化铸币、巴洛克工程建筑、跨大西洋与跨太平洋航运组织、印刷出版、军事要塞设计及更精细的财政簿记和地图测绘。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included mature mercury-amalgamation silver refining, large-scale minting, Baroque engineering and construction, organized transatlantic and transpacific shipping, printing, military fortification design, and more refined fiscal bookkeeping and cartography.

strengths.zh: 新西班牙的优势在于庞大人口、丰富银矿、高度发达的首都与区域市场、成熟税制、强大教会网络以及连接欧洲、加勒比、安第斯与亚洲的全球贸易节点地位。
strengths.en: New Spain's strengths lay in its large population, rich silver mines, highly developed capital and regional markets, mature tax system, powerful ecclesiastical networks, and position as a global trade hub linking Europe, the Caribbean, the Andes, and Asia.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括社会等级僵化、种族与法权不平等、地方腐败、走私普遍、北方边疆成本高昂、经济过度依赖白银与帝国贸易政策，以及克里奥尔精英对半岛官僚的长期不满。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included rigid social hierarchy, racial and legal inequality, local corruption, widespread smuggling, the high cost of the northern frontier, heavy dependence on silver and imperial trade policy, and chronic creole resentment toward peninsular officeholders.

outlook.zh: 1750年的新西班牙总体稳定且富裕，短期前景良好。若白银产量持续、王室改革推进有序并能抵御英国海上压力，则仍将是西班牙帝国财政支柱；但长期看，社会不平等、行政集中化与帝国财政榨取将积累深层政治紧张。
outlook.en: In 1750 New Spain was broadly stable and prosperous, with a favorable short-term outlook. If silver output continued, royal reforms advanced in an orderly way, and British maritime pressure was contained, it would remain the fiscal pillar of the Spanish Empire; in the longer term, however, inequality, administrative centralization, and imperial extraction would build deep political tensions.

description.zh: 涵盖墨西哥、中美洲北部与加勒比部分地区的西班牙殖民核心政区。
description.en: Spain's core colonial jurisdiction covering Mexico, northern Central America, and parts of the Caribbean.

ruler.zh: 何塞·安东尼奥·曼索·德·贝拉斯科

governmentForm.zh: 由西班牙王室任命总督统治的殖民总督辖区，结合王室官僚、教会法权与地方市政机构
governmentForm.en: A colonial viceroyalty governed by a royal appointee of the Spanish Crown, combining royal bureaucracy, ecclesiastical authority, and municipal institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈严格等级化，由半岛西班牙人、克里奥尔人、梅斯蒂索人、原住民共同体、非洲奴隶及自由有色人种构成；安第斯地区还保留以阿尤为基础的原住民村社秩序
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, consisting of peninsular Spaniards, creoles, mestizos, indigenous communities, African slaves, and free people of color; in the Andes, indigenous village order based on the ayllu also remained important

rulingClass.zh: 总督、审问法院法官、高级教士、半岛出身官员、大矿主与利马商人寡头
rulingClass.en: The viceroy, audiencia magistrates, senior clergy, peninsular officials, major mine owners, and the merchant oligarchy of Lima

succession.zh: 非世袭，由西班牙国王任命总督；地方高官亦由王室委任，关键职位受王室与印第亚斯委员会控制
succession.en: Non-hereditary; the viceroy was appointed by the King of Spain, while major offices were also filled by royal appointment under the supervision of the Crown and the Council of the Indies

structure.zh: 政府以利马总督为核心，下辖利马审问法院，并通过王室财政官、总司库、地方总督、科雷希多尔、市政会和教区网络治理广阔领土；1750年时南方上秘鲁仍属秘鲁总督辖区统辖
structure.en: Government centered on the viceroy in Lima, supported by the Audiencia of Lima, royal treasury officials, accountants, provincial governors, corregidores, cabildos, and a parish network across a vast territory; in 1750 Upper Peru in the south still belonged to the Viceroyalty of Peru

function.zh: 统筹政务、军务、司法协调与王室命令执行
function.en: Coordinated civil administration, military affairs, judicial oversight, and execution of royal orders

function.zh: 负责高级司法审判、行政咨议与对地方官员的监督
function.en: Handled high-level judicial cases, advised on governance, and supervised provincial officials

function.zh: 征收矿税、销售税、贡赋、垄断收入并管理国库
function.en: Collected mining taxes, sales taxes, tribute, monopoly revenues, and managed the treasury

name.zh: 地方科雷希多尔与总督区行政

function.zh: 管理省份、原住民村社、治安、劳役征调与地方税赋
function.en: Administered provinces, indigenous communities, public order, labor drafts, and local taxation

function.zh: 监管卡亚俄及太平洋贸易、海关、船运与违禁贸易稽查
function.en: Supervised Callao and Pacific trade, customs, shipping, and anti-smuggling enforcement

name.zh: 教会与传教事务协理机构

function.zh: 协调主教区、堂区、教育、施济与边疆传教站事务
function.en: Coordinated dioceses, parishes, education, poor relief, and frontier missions

function.zh: 监督波托西等矿区生产、汞供应、劳役配额与矿山纠纷
function.en: Oversaw production in mining zones such as Potosi, mercury supply, labor quotas, and mining disputes

function.zh: 负责城市供给、道路、市场监管、地方治安与公共工程
function.en: Managed urban provisioning, roads, market regulation, local policing, and public works

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以省级总督区、科雷希米恩托和城市市政会为主；原住民村社由库拉卡、酋长或社区长老在殖民法律框架下协助征贡与组织劳役
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on provincial governorships, corregimientos, and urban cabildos; indigenous communities were managed with the cooperation of kurakas, caciques, or local elders who helped collect tribute and organize labor under colonial law

legalSystem.zh: 法律基础为卡斯蒂利亚法、印第亚斯法与王室敕令，教会法在婚姻、道德与宗教案件中具有重要地位；审判程序繁复，司法常受社会等级与贿赂影响
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on Castilian law, the Laws of the Indies, and royal decrees, while canon law held importance in marriage, morality, and religious matters; procedure was complex, and justice was often shaped by social rank and corruption

taxationSystem.zh: 财政依赖原住民贡赋、阿尔卡瓦拉销售税、矿业王室五分税或相关矿税、汞与烟酒等垄断收入、海关税以及印花税；劳役制度如米塔虽非直接税，却构成国家汲取资源的重要机制
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income depended on indigenous tribute, the alcabala sales tax, the royal fifth or related mining taxes, monopoly revenues from mercury and other goods, customs duties, and stamp taxes; labor drafts such as the mita, though not a direct tax, were a key mechanism of extraction

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为官方与主导宗教，由主教区、修会、堂区与宗教裁判体系支撑；同时民间广泛存在天主教与安第斯原住民信仰混合的宗教实践
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the official and dominant religion, supported by dioceses, religious orders, parishes, and inquisitorial institutions; at the popular level, syncretic practices blending Catholicism with Andean indigenous beliefs were widespread

philosophy.zh: 官方思想以天主教经院传统、王权神授与帝国法理为核心，但18世纪中叶亦逐渐受波旁改革、实用理性与启蒙式行政改良思潮影响
philosophy.en: Official thought centered on Catholic scholasticism, divine monarchy, and imperial legal theory, yet by the mid-eighteenth century it was increasingly influenced by Bourbon reformism, practical reason, and Enlightenment-style administrative improvement

writingSystem.zh: 行政与学术主要使用西班牙文拉丁字母书写，教会文书也使用拉丁文；克丘亚语和艾马拉语在传教、司法与地方治理中常以拉丁字母记录
writingSystem.en: Administration and scholarship primarily used Spanish in the Latin alphabet, while ecclesiastical writing also employed Latin; Quechua and Aymara were often recorded in Latin script for missionary work, justice, and local governance

culturalAchievements.zh: 利马和库斯科形成巴洛克宗教建筑、绘画与雕刻中心；安第斯殖民艺术结合欧洲技法与本地题材，大学教育、修道院学术、宗教节庆、圣乐与城市仪式文化发达
culturalAchievements.en: Lima and Cuzco developed into centers of Baroque religious architecture, painting, and sculpture; Andean colonial art fused European techniques with local themes, while universities, monastic scholarship, religious festivals, sacred music, and urban ceremonial culture flourished

languageFamily.zh: 统治语言属印欧语系罗曼语族西班牙语；主要地方语言包括克丘亚语和艾马拉语，另有众多原住民语言与非洲裔社群语言传统
languageFamily.en: The ruling language belonged to the Indo-European Romance family, namely Spanish; major local languages included Quechua and Aymara, alongside many indigenous tongues and Afro-descendant linguistic traditions

gdpDescription.zh: 经济总量在美洲殖民地中极为可观，其核心由上秘鲁与安第斯矿业、农业供给、区域贸易和利马商贸金融中介构成，但财富分布极端不均且对贵金属与强制劳役依赖甚深
gdpDescription.en: Its total economic output was very substantial by colonial American standards, driven by Upper Peruvian and Andean mining, agricultural supply, regional trade, and Lima's commercial-financial intermediation, though wealth was highly unequal and deeply dependent on precious metals and coerced labor

mainIndustries.zh: 白银开采与冶炼、汞供应、谷物与玉米农业、畜牧、纺织作坊、葡萄酒与白兰地生产、沿海港口贸易、骡马运输和地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Silver mining and refining, mercury supply, grain and maize agriculture, livestock raising, textile workshops, wine and brandy production, coastal port trade, mule transport, and local handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 白银、黄金、汞、可可、皮革、羊毛、纺织品、糖、葡萄酒、科卡叶、染料、木材以及自欧洲输入的布匹、铁器、纸张与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Silver, gold, mercury, cacao, hides, wool, textiles, sugar, wine, coca leaves, dyes, timber, and imported European cloth, iron goods, paper, and luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距巨大。利马商人、矿主与教会机构拥有大量地产、银两与信贷网络；普通西裔和梅斯蒂索手工业者资产有限；多数原住民家庭以土地使用权、牲畜和社区资源维生，现金持有很少
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was extremely unequal. Lima merchants, mine owners, and church institutions held substantial estates, silver reserves, and credit networks; ordinary Spaniards and mestizo artisans owned modest assets; most indigenous households relied on land use rights, livestock, and communal resources with very little cash on hand

economicSystem.zh: 属重商主义殖民经济，王室通过贸易垄断、税收、矿业监管和港口控制攫取财富；地方市场则由庄园、矿区、原住民村社和城市商人网络共同运转
economicSystem.en: It was a mercantilist colonial economy in which the Crown extracted wealth through trade controls, taxation, mining regulation, and port supervision, while local markets functioned through estates, mining camps, indigenous communities, and urban merchant networks

source.zh: 矿业税与冶炼相关收入

source.en: Judicial fines, confiscations, and miscellaneous revenue

category.zh: 王室汇解与帝国性支出

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备主要集中于利马与主要矿区金库，用于维持军饷、港防、王室周转和灾后重建。1746年利马—卡亚俄大地震后，财政虽受压，但在矿税与贸易恢复下仍保持稳定
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were concentrated in Lima and major mining treasuries, supporting military pay, port defense, royal liquidity, and reconstruction. Although the 1746 Lima-Callao earthquake strained finances, recovery in mining taxes and trade kept the treasury broadly stable

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调确保矿业税源、恢复地震后港市功能、严控走私并提高征收效率；波旁改革倾向加强王权集中与财政审计，但尚未完全重塑旧有地方利益格局
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized securing mining revenues, restoring port and urban functions after the earthquake, suppressing smuggling, and improving collection efficiency; Bourbon reforms pushed toward tighter royal centralization and audit, though they had not yet fully overturned entrenched local interests

description.zh: 驻守利马、卡亚俄及主要行政与沿海据点，装备火枪与刺刀，为总督辖区核心常备力量
description.en: Stationed in Lima, Callao, and major administrative and coastal posts, armed with muskets and bayonets, forming the core standing force of the viceroyalty

description.zh: 用于沿海与高地机动巡逻、传令、镇压骚乱和边疆警戒
description.en: Used for mobile patrols in coast and highlands, communications, suppression of unrest, and frontier vigilance

description.zh: 负责卡亚俄港、要塞、城市防御工事及主要军械库的火炮操作
description.en: Manned artillery at Callao, fortifications, urban defenses, and principal arsenals

description.zh: 由西裔、克里奥尔、梅斯蒂索及部分有色自由民组成，平时兼职，战时用于守城、海防和地方治安
description.en: Composed of Spaniards, creoles, mestizos, and some free people of color, serving part-time in peace and used in wartime for garrison duty, coastal defense, and local security

name.en: Coastal Guard and Port Defense Forces

description.zh: 负责港口巡防、小型舰艇运作、海盗警戒和海上补给保护
description.en: Responsible for port patrols, operation of small vessels, anti-privateer vigilance, and protection of maritime supply lines

commanderInChief.zh: 秘鲁总督何塞·安东尼奥·曼索·德·贝拉斯科
commanderInChief.en: Viceroy Jose Antonio Manso de Velasco

technology.zh: 军备属18世纪中叶西班牙殖民标准，使用燧发枪、火炮、要塞工事与帆船港防体系；相较欧洲本土一线军队，训练、补给和装备更新较慢，但足以应对海盗、地方叛乱与有限外部威胁
technology.en: Military technology matched mid-eighteenth-century Spanish colonial standards, using flintlock muskets, cannon, fortifications, and sail-based port defenses; compared with front-line European armies, training, supply, and equipment renewal were slower, but adequate against privateers, local rebellions, and limited external threats

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自英国或其他欧洲海上敌国的袭扰、走私网络、安第斯高地潜在原住民起事以及南部与马普切边疆的长期不稳定
threats.en: Main threats came from British or other European maritime enemies, smuggling networks, potential indigenous uprisings in the Andean highlands, and long-term instability on the southern frontier facing Mapuche territories

populationDescription.zh: 人口以安第斯原住民为多数，集中于高地村社与旧印加核心区；另有西班牙裔、克里奥尔人、梅斯蒂索人、非洲裔奴隶与自由民，以及沿海和城市中的混合社群
populationDescription.en: The population was majority indigenous Andean, concentrated in highland communities and former Inca core regions; there were also Spaniards, creoles, mestizos, Afro-descended slaves and freed people, and mixed communities in coastal and urban areas

socialClasses.zh: 上层为半岛官僚、主教、矿主和大商人；中层包括克里奥尔地主、律师、下级教士、军官和手工业行会成员；底层则为原住民贡民、矿工、庄园劳工、梅斯蒂索城市贫民、奴隶与自由有色人群
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of peninsular bureaucrats, bishops, mine owners, and great merchants; the middle strata included creole landholders, lawyers, lower clergy, officers, and guild artisans; the lower strata comprised tributary indigenous people, miners, estate laborers, mestizo urban poor, slaves, and free people of color

allies.zh: 波旁西班牙帝国体系内的西属新西班牙及其他西属美洲辖区，以及在王朝框架下的法国波旁盟友
allies.en: The wider Bourbon Spanish imperial system, including New Spain and other Spanish American jurisdictions, as well as Bourbon France within the dynastic framework

enemies.zh: 主要潜在敌人为英国及其私掠力量，也包括走私商人与任何试图削弱西班牙太平洋统治的外部势力；南部边疆上则长期戒备马普切诸部
enemies.en: The principal potential enemy was Great Britain and its privateering forces, along with smugglers and any outside powers seeking to weaken Spanish control of the Pacific; on the southern frontier there was also long-standing caution toward Mapuche groups

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护西班牙在南太平洋与安第斯的垄断统治为目标，重点保护白银航线、管制港口贸易、压制走私并维持与其他西属殖民地的帝国内协调
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve Spanish monopoly rule in the South Pacific and Andes, focusing on protecting silver routes, controlling port commerce, suppressing smuggling, and maintaining imperial coordination with other Spanish colonies

era.zh: 启蒙时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括汞齐法白银提炼、殖民巴洛克建筑工程、要塞设计、跨安第斯骡运体系、大学与修会教育网络、地震后城市重建技术，以及逐步引入的波旁式行政统计与工程测绘
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included silver refining by mercury amalgamation, colonial Baroque construction, fortification design, trans-Andean mule transport systems, university and religious educational networks, post-earthquake rebuilding techniques, and the gradual adoption of Bourbon-style administrative statistics and engineering surveys

strengths.zh: 其优势在于控制世界级白银产区、拥有利马这一行政与商业中心、教会组织深入社会、城市网络稳固且王室权威在1750年前后仍然有效
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in controlling world-class silver districts, possessing Lima as an administrative and commercial center, benefiting from a church structure deeply embedded in society, maintaining a solid urban network, and still enjoying effective royal authority around 1750

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括经济过度依赖矿业和强制征发、社会等级与族群矛盾尖锐、交通距离遥远、沿海易受外敌袭扰、地震灾害频繁，以及财政与行政中长期存在腐败和走私问题
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included excessive reliance on mining and coerced extraction, sharp social and ethnic hierarchies, vast transport distances, vulnerable coasts, recurrent earthquake disasters, and chronic corruption and smuggling in finance and administration

outlook.zh: 1750年的西属秘鲁总体稳定，并有能力在波旁改革推动下继续恢复港市与财政，但深层结构性紧张——尤其是对原住民贡赋、米塔和殖民垄断的依赖——预示未来数十年将面临更强烈的社会抗议与政治重组压力
outlook.en: Spanish Peru in 1750 was broadly stable and capable of further recovery of its ports and finances under Bourbon reform, but deeper structural tensions—especially dependence on indigenous tribute, the mita, and colonial monopoly—foreshadowed stronger social protest and political pressures for reorganization in the decades ahead

description.zh: 安第斯中部的西班牙总督区，依托矿业、教会与殖民城市体系统治。
description.en: A Spanish viceroyalty in the central Andes ruled through mining, the church, and a network of colonial cities.

ruler.zh: 葡萄牙王室总督政府（名义宗主为若昂五世）
ruler.en: Governors-General of the Portuguese Crown (nominal sovereign John V of Portugal)

governmentForm.zh: 葡萄牙王室直辖的殖民总督制，结合总督、总审法院、主教区与地方市政议会的分层统治
governmentForm.en: A Portuguese Crown colonial governorship combining viceregal authority, high courts, bishoprics, and municipal councils under royal oversight

socialStructure.zh: 社会等级高度分层，由欧洲出生官员与地主、巴西本地白人种植园主、自由有色人、城市手工业者、土著社群与占人口大宗的非洲奴隶构成
socialStructure.en: A sharply stratified society composed of European-born officials and landowners, Brazil-born white planters, free people of color, urban artisans, indigenous communities, and a large enslaved African majority in many regions

rulingClass.zh: 王室任命官员、殖民高等神职人员、糖业与矿业寡头、地方市政精英
rulingClass.en: Crown-appointed officials, senior colonial clergy, sugar and mining magnates, and municipal elites

succession.zh: 总督由葡萄牙国王任命并可撤换，殖民地本身无独立世袭继承权
succession.en: The governor-general was appointed and removed by the Portuguese king; the colony itself had no independent hereditary succession

structure.zh: 1750年的葡属巴西仍以巴伊亚总督区为政治中心，由总督代表王权统辖主要海岸辖区；并与里约热内卢及南部军政区、各都督辖区、主教区、皇家财政机构和地方市议会共同运作。部分内陆边疆依赖军事指挥官、矿区监察官和传教网络维系统治。
structure.en: In 1750 Portuguese Brazil remained politically centered on the Governorate of Bahia, where the governor-general represented royal authority over the principal coastal jurisdictions. Administration functioned alongside Rio de Janeiro and southern military districts, captaincies, bishoprics, royal fiscal offices, and municipal councils. Interior frontier zones were governed more loosely through military commanders, mining inspectors, and missionary networks.

function.zh: 处理王室敕令、殖民政务协调、军事调度与对各都督辖区的监督
function.en: Handled royal orders, colonial coordination, military dispatches, and supervision of the captaincies

function.zh: 征收什一税、关税、矿业五分税、印花税及管理王室垄断收入
function.en: Collected tithes, customs, the mining royal fifth, stamp duties, and revenues from royal monopolies

function.zh: 审理重大民事与刑事案件，监督下级司法，维护殖民法律秩序
function.en: Heard major civil and criminal cases, supervised lower courts, and upheld colonial legal order

function.zh: 监管米纳斯吉拉斯等矿区的黄金开采、税收征解、铸币与走私查缉
function.en: Oversaw gold extraction in Minas Gerais and other mining zones, tax collection, minting, and anti-smuggling enforcement

function.zh: 管理萨尔瓦多、累西腓、里约热内卢等港口的进出口、航运登记与反私贸易
function.en: Managed imports and exports, shipping registration, and anti-smuggling operations in ports such as Salvador, Recife, and Rio de Janeiro

function.zh: 协调主教区、修会、教区登记、婚姻与土著传教聚落事务
function.en: Coordinated bishoprics, religious orders, parish records, marriage matters, and indigenous mission settlements

function.zh: 负责城市警务、道路、市场监管、地方征派与精英自治
function.en: Managed urban policing, roads, market regulation, local levies, and elite self-government

function.zh: 统筹要塞、民兵、边疆驻军、海防和对西属边界与土著地区的防御
function.en: Coordinated forts, militia, frontier garrisons, coastal defense, and protection along Spanish borders and indigenous zones

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以都督辖区、市政议会和教区为基本单元。沿海核心区由王室控制较强，矿区则通过矿务总监与财政官强化征税，内陆边疆多借助地主、军事首领、传教士与承包人进行间接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on captaincies, municipal councils, and parishes. Royal control was strongest in the core coastal districts, while mining regions were more tightly taxed through intendants and treasury officers. Much of the interior frontier was ruled indirectly through landholders, military chiefs, missionaries, and contractors.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以葡萄牙王国法、王室敕令、教会法及殖民地特别规章为基础。司法实践因地区差异很大，奴隶制、种族身份、财产资格和教会审查深刻影响审判结果。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on Portuguese royal law, crown ordinances, canon law, and special colonial regulations. Judicial practice varied widely by region, and slavery, racial status, property qualifications, and ecclesiastical oversight strongly influenced outcomes.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以黄金五分税、进出口关税、什一税、印花税、盐烟酒等专卖或包税收入、港口费用及地方杂税为主。矿区征收最重，沿海贸易口岸则依赖关税和航运收费。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on the royal fifth on gold, customs duties, tithes, stamp taxes, monopoly or farmed revenues on goods such as salt and tobacco, port fees, and various local levies. Mining districts bore the heaviest burdens, while coastal ports depended more on customs and shipping charges.

religion.zh: 官方宗教为罗马天主教，受王室保教权制度支配；但民间广泛存在非洲宗教实践、民间圣徒崇拜、土著传统与天主教混合信仰
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the official religion under the royal patronage system, but African religious practices, popular saint devotion, indigenous traditions, and syncretic forms of belief were widespread

philosophy.zh: 主流思想仍以反宗教改革时期的天主教经院传统、王权忠诚与等级秩序观为核心；同时经由科英布拉、耶稣会与大西洋商路逐渐接触启蒙时代的自然法、重商主义与实用科学观念
philosophy.en: Mainstream thought remained rooted in Counter-Reformation Catholic scholasticism, loyalty to monarchy, and hierarchical social order, though natural law, mercantilist ideas, and practical scientific concepts of the Enlightenment increasingly entered through Coimbra, the Jesuits, and Atlantic trade networks

writingSystem.zh: 主要文字系统为葡萄牙语拉丁字母书写；教会与行政档案使用规范葡萄牙文，部分传教区也记录土著语言与通用语
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was Portuguese written in the Latin alphabet; official and ecclesiastical records used standard Portuguese, while some missions also recorded indigenous languages and trade vernaculars

culturalAchievements.zh: 18世纪中叶的葡属巴西在巴洛克艺术、宗教建筑、金银圣器、木雕与音乐上成就显著。米纳斯吉拉斯和巴伊亚地区发展出独特殖民巴洛克风格，城市节庆、兄弟会组织、非洲鼓乐传统与甘蔗、矿业社会形成的混合文化尤为鲜明。
culturalAchievements.en: By the mid-eighteenth century Portuguese Brazil had achieved notable distinction in Baroque art, religious architecture, gold and silver liturgical craft, woodcarving, and sacred music. Minas Gerais and Bahia developed distinctive colonial Baroque styles, while urban festivals, lay brotherhoods, African musical traditions, and the mixed culture of sugar and mining society were especially prominent.

languageFamily.zh: 主要行政语言属罗曼语族西伊比利亚支的葡萄牙语；同时存在图皮通用语、非洲语言群与多种土著语言
languageFamily.en: The main administrative language was Portuguese, a Western Iberian Romance language; Tupi-based lingua francas, African language groups, and numerous indigenous languages were also present

unit.zh: 葡萄牙雷伊斯（按千雷伊斯计）

gdpDescription.zh: 葡属巴西在1750年是大西洋世界最富有的殖民地之一。经济由东北糖业、东南金矿、畜牧业、烟草和港口贸易共同驱动。矿业繁荣虽已开始从峰值回落，但仍为王室和殖民精英提供巨额财富。
gdpDescription.en: In 1750 Portuguese Brazil was one of the wealthiest colonies in the Atlantic world. Its economy was driven by northeastern sugar, southeastern gold mining, cattle raising, tobacco, and port commerce. Although mining had begun to decline from its peak, it still generated immense wealth for the crown and colonial elites.

mainIndustries.zh: 甘蔗种植与制糖、黄金和钻石开采、烟草、畜牧业、奴隶贸易转运、航运、木材采伐、皮革和地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Sugar cultivation and refining, gold and diamond extraction, tobacco, cattle ranching, slave-trade redistribution, shipping, timber extraction, hides, and local artisan production

tradeGoods.zh: 出口糖、金、烟草、皮革、巴西木、棉花和部分钻石；进口奴隶、纺织品、葡萄酒、橄榄油、铁器、奢侈品和制成品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included sugar, gold, tobacco, hides, brazilwood, cotton, and some diamonds; imports included enslaved people, textiles, wine, olive oil, iron goods, luxury items, and manufactured wares

householdWealth.zh: 财富分布极端不均。种植园主、矿主、商人和官僚积累大量土地、奴隶和贵金属，而大多数自由贫民几乎没有稳定资产，奴隶则被视为财产本身。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was extremely unequal. Planters, mine owners, merchants, and officials held substantial land, slave property, and precious metals, while most free poor possessed little stable property and enslaved people were treated as property themselves.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制属殖民重商主义。王室通过航运控制、关税、专卖、特许贸易与矿税吸取收益，生产依赖大庄园、奴隶劳动、矿业许可和跨大西洋信用网络。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was colonial mercantilism. The crown extracted revenue through shipping controls, customs, monopolies, chartered trade, and mining taxes, while production relied on large estates, enslaved labor, mining licenses, and transatlantic credit networks.

source.zh: 黄金五分税与矿区相关收入
source.en: Royal fifth on gold and mining-related revenues
Monopolies, tax farming, and stamp duties

category.zh: 教会、传教与教育性支出
对里斯本汇缴与债务支付
category.en: Transfers to Lisbon and debt payments

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民财政库存在账面上较为充裕，部分以金币、银币、金锭和待运税金形式保存在萨尔瓦多、里约热内卢及矿区财政所。但由于运输风险、走私和对宗主国汇解，实际可自由支配部分有限。
treasuryDescription.en: The colonial treasury appeared fairly strong on paper, with reserves held in coin, bullion, and unshipped tax receipts at Salvador, Rio de Janeiro, and mining treasuries. Yet the truly disposable portion was constrained by transport risk, smuggling, and remittances owed to the metropolis.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以榨取矿业和贸易收益、维持驻军和港口、防止走私并确保对葡萄牙本土输送贵金属为核心。王室在繁荣时期倾向维持高税负，但也偶尔以减免和包税方式稳住地方精英。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on extracting mining and trade revenues, maintaining garrisons and ports, combating smuggling, and ensuring the flow of precious metals to Portugal. In prosperous years the crown favored heavy extraction, though it sometimes used exemptions and tax farming to secure elite cooperation.

description.zh: 由葡萄牙本土与殖民地招募组成，驻守萨尔瓦多、里约热内卢、累西腓及主要要塞和行政中心
description.en: Recruited from Portugal and the colony, stationed in Salvador, Rio de Janeiro, Recife, and major forts and administrative centers

description.zh: 由白人、自由有色人及部分混血居民编成，承担地方治安、边疆巡逻和战时增援
description.en: Formed from whites, free people of color, and some mixed-race inhabitants, serving in local security, frontier patrol, and wartime reinforcement

description.zh: 主要活跃于南部边疆和内陆牧区，用于快速通信、边境侦察和镇压骚乱
description.en: Active mainly on the southern frontier and inland ranching zones, used for rapid communication, border reconnaissance, and suppression of unrest

description.zh: 负责港口炮台、海岸堡垒、舰船火炮和主要城市海防警戒
description.en: Responsible for harbor batteries, coastal forts, naval guns, and maritime defense of the principal cities

description.zh: 在特定边疆和传教区协同作战，熟悉丛林、河道与内陆交通环境
description.en: Served in particular frontier and mission zones, with strong knowledge of forests, rivers, and inland routes

commanderInChief.zh: 总督兼殖民地最高军事长官
commanderInChief.en: The Governor-General as supreme military authority in the colony

technology.zh: 军备属18世纪中期大西洋殖民标准，装备燧发枪、火炮、要塞炮台和少量武装船只。军纪和训练优于多数土著对手，但不及欧洲本土一线军队，补给高度依赖海路与地方征发。
technology.en: Military technology matched mid-eighteenth-century Atlantic colonial standards, with flintlock muskets, artillery, fortress batteries, and a small number of armed vessels. Discipline and training were superior to most indigenous opponents, but below first-rate European armies, and logistics depended heavily on sea routes and local requisition.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括与西属南美边界的冲突、港口遭英法或私掠船袭击、矿区与种植园奴隶反抗、土著边疆战争及广泛走私对王室控制的侵蚀。
threats.en: Main threats included border conflict with Spanish South America, attacks on ports by British, French, or privateering forces, slave resistance in mines and plantations, indigenous frontier warfare, and the erosion of royal control through widespread smuggling.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约三百万，是18世纪中叶美洲最大的殖民社会之一。人口分布极不均衡，东北沿海糖区、巴伊亚、伯南布哥、里约热内卢及米纳斯吉拉斯矿区最为密集，广阔内陆仍相对稀疏。非洲出生或后裔人口占极高比重，奴隶制塑造了整体社会结构。
populationDescription.en: With roughly three million inhabitants, Portuguese Brazil was one of the largest colonial societies in the Americas by the mid-eighteenth century. Population was unevenly distributed, concentrating in the northeastern sugar coast, Bahia, Pernambuco, Rio de Janeiro, and the mining zone of Minas Gerais, while much of the interior remained sparsely settled. Africans and people of African descent formed a very large share of the population, and slavery shaped the entire social order.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大致包括王室与教会精英、大种植园主和矿主、商人与承包税商、中小地主与自由职业者、自由有色人、城市贫民、土著村社成员，以及构成劳动力核心的奴隶人口。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included crown and ecclesiastical elites, large planters and mine owners, merchants and tax farmers, middling landholders and professionals, free people of color, the urban poor, members of indigenous village communities, and the enslaved population that formed the core of the labor force.

allies.zh: 正式盟友为宗主国葡萄牙王国及其大西洋体系；在更广泛国际关系中，葡萄牙长期依赖与英国的合作与贸易联系间接维护巴西安全
allies.en: Its formal ally was the Kingdom of Portugal and its Atlantic imperial system; in broader international relations Portuguese security indirectly depended on longstanding cooperation and trade ties with Great Britain

enemies.zh: 主要对手为波旁西班牙在拉普拉塔和亚马孙边界的殖民力量，此外英国、法国和荷兰私掠者与商人也是持续性的竞争与威胁来源
enemies.en: Its principal rival was Bourbon Spain in the La Plata and Amazonian borderlands, while British, French, and Dutch privateers and traders were continuing sources of competition and danger

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策由里斯本主导，目标是保住巴西金矿、稳固海岸港口、扩展对内陆边疆的实际占领，并通过谈判与驻军把既成拓殖范围合法化。1750年马德里条约正体现这一以实际控制换取边界承认的方针。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed from Lisbon and aimed to secure Brazilian gold, protect the coastal ports, expand effective occupation of the interior frontier, and convert de facto settlement into recognized sovereignty through negotiation and garrisoning. The Treaty of Madrid in 1750 exemplified this policy of trading on-the-ground occupation for border recognition.

era.zh: 启蒙时代早中期的重商主义殖民技术阶段
era.en: The early to middle Enlightenment era of mercantilist colonial technology

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术和实践包括大西洋远洋航运、糖厂机械化流程、矿井排水与淘洗技术、殖民堡垒工程、港口仓储体系、金锭与铸币管理，以及在热带农业、地图测绘和边疆探险方面的经验积累。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and practices included Atlantic blue-water shipping, mechanized sugar-mill processing, mine drainage and washing techniques, colonial fortification engineering, port warehousing systems, bullion and mint management, and growing practical knowledge in tropical agriculture, mapping, and frontier exploration.

strengths.zh: 葡属巴西的优势在于辽阔土地、丰富黄金与农业资源、重要港口网络、不断扩展的定居前沿和与大西洋贸易体系的深度整合。其财政产出对葡萄牙王室具有决定性意义。
strengths.en: Portuguese Brazil's strengths lay in its vast territory, rich gold and agricultural resources, important port network, expanding settlement frontier, and deep integration into the Atlantic trading system. Its fiscal output was of decisive importance to the Portuguese crown.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点同样明显：经济高度依赖奴隶制和少数出口商品，行政控制地域不均，走私严重，边疆漫长，社会不平等尖锐，且对宗主国航运和欧洲市场需求极为敏感。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were equally clear: heavy dependence on slavery and a narrow export base, uneven administrative control, extensive smuggling, long frontiers, acute social inequality, and strong dependence on metropolitan shipping and European market demand.

outlook.zh: 1750年的前景总体乐观但潜藏紧张。若王室能继续控制矿税、改善边疆治理并维持海上通道，殖民地仍将繁荣；但矿产递减、行政改革压力、奴隶制矛盾和地区精英对王权汲取的不满，已为未来的不稳定埋下伏笔。
outlook.en: The outlook in 1750 was broadly favorable but contained underlying tension. If the crown could continue controlling mining revenue, improve frontier governance, and keep sea lanes secure, the colony would remain prosperous; yet mineral decline, pressure for reform, contradictions of slavery, and elite resentment of royal extraction were already laying the groundwork for future instability.

description.zh: 葡萄牙在南美的广大殖民地，以糖业、金矿与奴隶贸易推动扩张。
description.en: Portugal's vast South American colony, expanded through sugar, gold mining, and the slave trade.

ruler.zh: 诸隆科首领与托基战时领袖
ruler.en: Various lonkos and toki war leaders

rulerTitle.zh: 隆科（酋长）、托基（战时统帅）

governmentForm.zh: 以地方氏族与地域阿伊亚雷韦、布塔尔马普联合而成的部族邦联；平时分散自治，战时推举托基统一指挥
governmentForm.en: A tribal confederation formed by local kin groups and territorial aillarehue and butalmapu leagues; decentralized in peacetime, with a toki elected for unified command in war

socialStructure.zh: 社会以家族、父系亲族网络与地方共同体为基础，贵望家族首领、勇士、自由家户、附属者与俘虏构成层级，但整体较周边君主国家更分散且协商性更强
socialStructure.en: Society was based on households, patrilineal kin networks, and local communities, with prestigious leading families, warriors, free households, dependents, and captives forming a hierarchy that remained more decentralized and negotiated than neighboring monarchies

rulingClass.zh: 地方隆科家族、战功卓著的勇士集团、主持议会与联盟的区域首领
rulingClass.en: Local lonko lineages, distinguished warrior groups, and regional leaders who convened councils and alliances

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在显赫家族内继承，但需获得亲族与追随者认可；托基则在危机或战争中由议会推举，非固定世袭
succession.en: Chiefly status usually passed within prominent families but required the acceptance of kin and followers; the toki was chosen by council during crisis or war and was not permanently hereditary

structure.zh: 无集中官僚国家。治理依靠地方隆科、长者会议、宗亲网络与跨地区议会；较大区域可分为拉夫肯切、纳格切、瓦尔切与佩文切等群体，战时通过布塔尔马普层级协调
structure.en: There was no centralized bureaucratic state. Governance relied on local lonkos, councils of elders, kinship networks, and interregional assemblies; larger zones included groups such as the Lafkenche, Nagche, Wenteche, and Pehuenche, coordinated in wartime through butalmapu-level alliances

function.zh: 处理土地、婚姻、纠纷调停、对外关系与日常共同体事务
function.en: Handled land matters, marriage arrangements, dispute mediation, external relations, and routine community affairs

function.zh: 召集跨地区协商，决定战争、和约、边境交涉与共同防务
function.en: Convened interregional deliberations to decide war, truces, frontier negotiations, and common defense

function.zh: 在战时组织军力、调集骑兵、制定突袭与边境防御计划
function.en: Organized military forces in wartime, mobilized cavalry, and planned raids and frontier defense

function.zh: 维持与西班牙边境驻军、传教士、商人及邻近土著群体的往来与谈判
function.en: Maintained contact and negotiation with Spanish frontier garrisons, missionaries, merchants, and neighboring indigenous groups

name.zh: 礼仪与法律习惯主持者
name.en: Custodians of Ritual and Customary Law

function.zh: 主持盟誓、赔偿、和解仪式，并维护习惯法与祖传规范
function.en: Oversaw oaths, compensation, and reconciliation rituals, while preserving customary law and ancestral norms

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以分散聚落、家族单位与地区首领协商为核心，并不存在按欧洲或东亚国家模式设置的常设州县官署
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on dispersed settlements, household groupings, and negotiation among regional leaders; there were no permanent provincial offices in the European or East Asian state sense

legalSystem.zh: 以习惯法、口头传统、赔偿原则与议会裁断为主。杀伤、婚姻、牲畜争端、俘虏处置等事务通常通过谈判、补偿和公开见证解决
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on customary law, oral tradition, compensation principles, and council judgment. Homicide, marriage, livestock disputes, and captive matters were commonly resolved through negotiation, indemnity, and public witnessing

taxationSystem.zh: 无统一常设税制。首领依亲族义务、馈赠、战利品分配、牲畜调集与临时供给维持权威；与西班牙边境贸易中的交换收益亦重要
taxationSystem.en: There was no uniform permanent tax system. Leaders sustained authority through kin obligations, gift exchange, distribution of war booty, livestock mobilization, and temporary provisioning; gains from frontier trade with the Spanish were also important

religion.zh: 传统马普切宇宙观与祖灵、自然力量信仰占主导，马奇承担治病、占卜与祭仪职能；部分边境地区亦接触天主教，但多为有限融合而非完全改宗
religion.en: Traditional Mapuche cosmology and belief in ancestral and natural spiritual forces predominated, with machi serving as healers, diviners, and ritual specialists; some frontier areas also had contact with Catholicism, though usually through limited syncretism rather than full conversion

philosophy.zh: 重视共同体荣誉、亲族义务、口头记忆、与土地和祖先的联系，以及在议会中通过协商维持平衡与自主
philosophy.en: Core values emphasized communal honor, kin obligation, oral memory, ties to land and ancestors, and the maintenance of balance and autonomy through council deliberation

writingSystem.zh: 无本土广泛使用的文字行政体系，知识主要依靠口头传承；与西班牙接触后，部分条约、传教文献与词汇记录以西班牙字母书写马普敦贡语
writingSystem.en: There was no indigenous widely used administrative writing system, and knowledge was transmitted mainly orally; after contact with the Spanish, some treaties, missionary texts, and lexical records wrote Mapudungun in the Spanish alphabet

culturalAchievements.zh: 在阿劳科战争长期抗争中形成高度韧性的边境文化、精熟骑术与机动战法、复杂的议会传统、丰富的口头诗歌与仪式音乐，并成功吸收马匹、金属器与跨边境贸易品以重塑社会生活
culturalAchievements.en: The Mapuche developed a highly resilient frontier culture through the long Arauco War, mastering horsemanship and mobile warfare, sustaining complex council traditions, preserving rich oral poetry and ritual music, and effectively incorporating horses, metal tools, and cross-border trade goods into social life

languageFamily.zh: 马普切语系（马普敦贡语）
languageFamily.en: Mapuche language family (Mapudungun)

unit.en: Spanish silver pesos per person (estimated equivalent)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业、畜牧、狩猎采集、边境交换与战利品再分配为主。1750年前后，马普切社会已深度适应马匹与牲畜经济，财富多以牲畜、金属器、纺织品和俘虏劳力体现，而非国家财政货币统计
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on subsistence agriculture, pastoralism, hunting and gathering, frontier exchange, and redistribution of spoils. By around 1750, Mapuche society had deeply adapted to a horse-and-livestock economy, and wealth was expressed more in herds, metal goods, textiles, and captive labor than in state monetary accounting

mainIndustries.zh: 农业（玉米、豆类、藜麦、马铃薯、小麦在部分地区已传播）、牧马与养牛、纺织、皮革加工、木工、银饰制作、边境贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture (maize, beans, quinoa, potatoes, and in some areas adopted wheat), horse and cattle raising, weaving, leatherworking, woodworking, silver ornament production, and frontier trade

tradeGoods.zh: 马匹、牛羊、皮革、织物、毯子、盐、木制品、银饰、俘虏劳力；输入品包括铁器、刀具、酒类、玻璃珠、火器、布匹与其他西班牙边境商品
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included horses, cattle and sheep, hides, textiles, ponchos, salt, wooden products, silver ornaments, and captive labor; imports included iron tools, blades, alcohol, glass beads, firearms, cloth, and other Spanish frontier goods

name.zh: 以物易物兼用西班牙银币
name.en: Barter with supplementary Spanish silver coin

unitName.en: Livestock, textiles, and pesos (in parallel use)
